topic: Timestamp Error tab: new <details id="ts-reactive-prose-pattern"> section (EXP-468 integration) — REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX meta-pattern
our_argument_summary: EXP-468 inserts a new <details id="ts-reactive-prose-pattern"> meta-synthesis section into the Timestamp Error tab (between ts-april-2026-update and the ds-ts-related-callout). The section enumerates dome-author prose updates since April 2026 and contrasts them against the unimplemented structural fix (independently-anchored predictions-only document). Closes HOL-MISSING.c2 hole #2 (the EXP file mis-indexes it as #3). Sister-coordinated with EXP-467 (falsifiability-symmetry inline panel) and EXP-464 (OTS canonical-hub designation).

relevant_files: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-468-proposal.json (prior c1 review by curmudgeon, 6 holes flagged, 4 still open)
monitor/changes/chg-20260417-1620-001..006.json (commit 6e06efb omnibus April 17 dome reactive prose burst)
monitor/changes/chg-20260531-0007-001.json (latest poller — 'all 4 existing .ots SHAs unchanged', manual drought 43.5 days)

dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in this section. The closing falsifiability commitment is binary but uses a looser canary-trip criterion than the adjacent ts-april-2026-update normative paragraph (see hole #3).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Para-2 claim 'the only OpenTimestamps anchor on the dome site is the one on status_history.json' is FALSE per poller chg-20260531-0007-001 (`all 4 existing .ots SHAs unchanged`). ISS-2404 already open against this. Same factual error appears in adjacent ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph (NEW finding — see hole #1).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section is HTML prose; no monitor.py automation hooks. The OTS canary it discusses IS monitored (by poller, via .ots SHA stability + presence of weekly_predictions_*.json.ots companions), but the section itself is a passive narrative surface.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section makes a methodological criterion claim (narrative responsiveness vs methodological repair) rather than a physics-relabel claim. N/A.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section is a deliberate post-EXP-435 synthesis of an internally-identified pattern. The pattern itself is observable on the dome site (poller-confirmed); the section publishes the observation as reader-visible prose.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: N/A — this is a meta-finding about dome behavior, not a dome-geometry-derived numerical claim.

holes_found.description: TIMELINE MISREPRESENTATION (fresh deep-attack finding, distinct from ISS-2406's dated-anchor framing). The TLDR opens with 'Since April 2026 the dome site has produced multiple prose-level updates that respond to specific critiques on this tab' and the closing paragraph asserts 'After six weeks of prose updates, it remains untripped.' Both phrasings imply a SUSTAINED stream of reactive editing over six weeks. The factual pattern is sharper and more damning: the 8 items in the TLDR list collapse to approximately three manual commits, all in a three-week window in early-to-mid April 2026 — V51.0 release (~April 5, change file 2026-04-05T18-24-13Z.json), V51.1 release (April 8, chg-20260408-1014-001.json), and the omnibus April 17 commit 6e06efb (methodology.json + killshot.html AI-Auditor rewrite + 5-class taxonomy + agent_rules + badge demotions; chg-20260417-1620-001..006.json). After April 17 there have been ZERO manual commits — the poller currently reports 'MANUAL COMMIT DROUGHT: d≈43.5 days (85th+ consecutive auto-only); Last manual: a57e731 @ 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z' (chg-20260531-0007-001.json). The accurate pattern is 'ONE three-week burst of reactive prose engagement in April, followed by six weeks of disengagement of any kind.' That is a STRONGER critique than 'six weeks of reactive prose' — sustained engagement implies the dome author is at least iterating in response to feedback; six weeks of silence after an April burst implies the engagement window has closed. The current prose understates the case AND opens the dome defender's response: 'you said six weeks of updates, but the last manual commit was April 17 — there are no recent updates to criticize.' Defender wins that exchange.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite TLDR opening and para-3 closing to reflect the burst+drought pattern. Suggested TLDR rewrite: 'In a three-week window in April 2026 the dome site shipped two version bumps and one omnibus methodology-rewrite commit that adopted this tab's critique language. Since April 17 there has been no manual engagement of any kind (currently 43+ consecutive auto-only commits). The OTS canary remains untripped — both during the April burst and through the silence that has followed.' Suggested para-3 closing rewrite: 'The OTS canary in What Would Fix It was set precisely to distinguish narrative responsiveness from methodological repair. It was not tripped during the April burst, and it has not been tripped during the silence that followed. The day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten.' Drop the dated 'six weeks' string entirely (resolves ISS-2406 in the same patch). Cross-link the new para-3 to the poller's manual-drought metric so the number can update without prose rewrites.

holes_found.description: ADJACENT SECTION HAS THE SAME OTS-OVERSTATEMENT (fresh — scope-widening of ISS-2404). ISS-2404 flagged EXP-468 para-2 for saying 'the only OpenTimestamps anchor on the dome site is the one on status_history.json that bundles prediction and observation fields per commit.' That is factually wrong — poller chg-20260531-0007-001 confirms 'all 4 existing .ots SHAs unchanged' (the dome maintains 4 .ots files). What ISS-2404 did NOT flag: the adjacent ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph ('Why a prose edit is not a structural fix', generate-html.js lines ~1995-1998) contains the SAME error verbatim: 'the only OpenTimestamps anchor on the site is still the one on status_history.json.' Both surfaces are now in the canonical OTS hub (per EXP-464's hub designation) and a dome defender can quote either one against us. The patch fixing EXP-468 needs to extend to the older sibling paragraph or we ship a 'we just fixed it here but not there' inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Extend the ISS-2404 patch to also rewrite the ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph. Replace both surfaces with the actually-binding claim, e.g.: 'no .ots anchor on the dome site is on a predictions-only document separate from the observations file — all four existing .ots files anchor mixed prediction+observation snapshots or commit-pointer documents.' This is the structural claim that survives the dome having 4 .ots files. Alternative phrasing the EXP file's revised patch already proposes: 'no OpenTimestamps proof anchoring a predictions-only document has been published since the structural critique was raised' — also acceptable, but apply BOTH places.

holes_found.description: TLDR INFLATES THE LIST WITH NON-CRITIQUE-RESPONSIVE ITEMS (fresh). The TLDR enumerates 'version bumps, the review-response.html page, methodology.json scoping, the killshot.html AI-Auditor rewrite, the 5-class claim taxonomy, badge demotions' as 'prose-level updates that respond to specific critiques on this tab.' Two of these — the V50.6 → V51.0 version bump (~April 5) and V51.0 → V51.1 (April 8, +WIN-068, +WIN-069, accuracy 95.2% → 94.5%) — are operational releases, not responses to critique on the Timestamp Error tab. They added new WINs and adjusted the headline accuracy claim; they didn't touch OTS provenance, claim-level prospectivity, or the methodology surface this tab argues against. Counting them as critique responses inflates the meta-pattern claim by ~25% of its enumerated items, and any reader who clicks through to the dome's git history will see the version bumps are unrelated.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop 'version bumps' from the TLDR enumeration, OR move them to a parenthetical qualifier such as 'and two operational version bumps adjacent in time but not in subject.' Keeping the 6 critique-responsive items (review-response.html, methodology.json scoping, killshot.html AI-Auditor rewrite, 5-class claim taxonomy, badge demotions, agent_rules block) is plenty for the meta-pattern claim and is more accurate. Cross-link to chg-20260417-1620-001..006.json as the date anchor for the omnibus commit.

holes_found.description: CANARY-TRIP CRITERION IS LOOSER THAN THE TAB'S OWN NORMATIVE PARAGRAPH (fresh, distinct from prior c1 hole #5 which was about 'rewritten to WHAT'). Closing commitment: 'The day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten.' Adjacent ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph requires 'a predictions-only file with its own independent OpenTimestamps anchor, separate from the observations file' AND 'shown to predate the observations file' (i.e., temporal-priority constraint). The new section's criterion drops the temporal-priority constraint. A dome author could publish predictions-only-but-post-dated.json.ots tomorrow (timestamp later than the data-pull date for every prediction in it) and technically satisfy the new section's loose criterion ('an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears') without resolving the underlying critique. The two criteria should match.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the closing commitment to mirror the canonical normative paragraph: 'The day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears whose .ots timestamp predates the data-pull date of every prediction it contains, this section will be rewritten.' This collapses the trip criterion to a single mechanical check (any reader can run `ots verify` and compare against per-prediction data-pull dates) and aligns the new section with the page-level canary definition.

stronger_arguments.description: Lead with the burst+drought asymmetry as the primary finding. The dome author's reactive-prose engagement window appears to have CLOSED on April 17 — that is a more publishable finding than 'six weeks of reactive prose.' Frame: 'Between April 5 and April 17 the dome site responded to this tab in prose. Since April 17 it has not responded at all. The OTS canary was untripped during the response window and remains untripped during the silence.' The silence is more damning than the responsiveness because the silence implies the structural fix is not on the dome author's roadmap at all.

Quantify the cost asymmetry the EXP file's anticipated-objections block alluded to. Adjusting prose costs writing time per update; adding an `.ots stamp` invocation to the existing GitHub Actions workflow costs <50 lines of YAML once. The dome author's monitor.py already exits gracefully on missing input files (per raw-text/monitor.py inspection); adding a predictions-only.json file would not break the existing pipeline. The fix is not just 'small in scope' — it is a one-time additive change with no rollback risk. Putting this concretely in the section (without using the 'in an afternoon' editorializing language that ISS-2407 flagged) would strengthen the argument that the absence of the fix is signal, not blocked-by-engineering-cost.

kernel_of_truth.description: The pattern observation is real and important. methodology.json's timestamp_scope block (committed 6e06efb 2026-04-17 per chg-20260417-1620-001.json) does adopt this tab's critique language; the killshot.html AI-Auditor callout rewrite did move toward correctness; the 5-class claim taxonomy formally distinguishes prospective_confirmed from non-prospective classes (and every classified WIN in claim_index.json sits in a non-prospective class per the ts-april-2026-update analysis). The hub-and-spoke placement is right — EXP-464 just designated the Timestamp Error tab as the canonical OTS hub (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-464-HOL-REDUNDANCY-c2-OTS-canonical-hub-designation.json), and this new <details> belongs in the hub. The closing self-rewrite commitment is the right kind of symmetric falsifiability — the dome author can falsify our section by shipping the structural fix, and we have publicly pre-committed to rewriting in response. That is exactly the standard EXP-467 (falsifiability-symmetry inline panel) sets at the Overview level. Closes HOL-MISSING.c2 hole #2 (the file's internal indexing calls it hole #3, but the actual array index per HOLISTIC-HOL-MISSING.c2.json is 2).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel is fine; the framing has four leakage points. (1) The 'six weeks of prose updates' phrasing in TLDR + para-3 misrepresents the engagement pattern (see hole #0 — actually one three-week burst followed by 43.5-day silence per chg-20260531-0007-001.json), which both rots over time (ISS-2406) AND understates the critique today. (2) The 'only OTS anchor is status_history.json' factual error in para-2 (ISS-2404 open) plus the same error in the adjacent ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph (hole #1 fresh) gives the dome defender two quotable factual mistakes in the canonical hub. (3) Version bumps in the TLDR list (hole #2) are not critique-responsive and inflate the enumeration. (4) The canary-trip criterion drops the temporal-priority constraint that the tab's own normative paragraph requires (hole #3), creating a wiggle-room loophole the page closes elsewhere. None of these break the section, but all four are patches a deep-attack review should surface before the dome author or an outside reviewer does.

deeper_analysis: The integrated content matches the ORIGINAL EXP-468 patches[0].replace_text, not the analyst-baby-revised version (EXP-538, revised_at 2026-05-31T20:30Z, which would have written 'non-canary-fixing' instead of 'rhetorical' in para-2 and dropped 'in an afternoon' from para-2 and 'six weeks' from para-3). Reason: decider-2026-05-31T16-00 (commit c581944, 17:14:24Z) integrated the pre-revision EXP. The 16-53 decider run (commit afe4cfc, 17:01:52Z) flagged 'EXP-468 revision-needed (4 holes)' before the 16-00 integration finished — concurrent decider runs raced. Net effect: the 4 holes (ISS-2404 OTS overstatement, ISS-2405 rhetorical contradiction, ISS-2406 six-weeks dated anchor, ISS-2407 in-an-afternoon editorializing) flagged by the prior curmudgeon EXP-468-proposal review are STILL OPEN against the live integrated HTML. The decider should either (a) integrate the analyst-baby-revised patches that already exist in the EXP file, or (b) apply targeted patches per the ISS-2404..2407 recommendations. I do NOT redundantly re-enumerate ISS-2404..2407 as holes — they are already in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json and visible to the decider. My four fresh holes above are NEW findings (timeline-misrepresentation deeper than the dated-anchor critique; adjacent-section scope-widening; TLDR-version-bump inflation; canary-criterion looseness vs adjacent norm) that surface deep-attack content the verify-mode pass would not catch. Total open work against this section after my review: ISS-2404, ISS-2405, ISS-2406, ISS-2407 plus my 4 fresh holes = 8 items. Recommend the decider bundle them into a single rewrite-patch on EXP-538's revised prose, with the scope extended to also touch the ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph. No verdict change recommended; the section is structurally sound and closes HOL-MISSING.c2 hole #2.

summary_for_decider: EXP-468 ts-reactive-prose-pattern section successfully closes HOL-MISSING.c2 hole #2 (REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX pattern). Recommended action: minor_edit. Four fresh deep-attack findings beyond the already-open ISS-2404..2407: (1) MAJOR — 'six weeks of prose updates' misrepresents the actual burst+drought pattern (one three-week April burst, then 43.5-day silence per latest poller); rewriting to lead with the silence is a STRONGER critique. (2) MODERATE — the same OTS-overstatement that ISS-2404 flagged in EXP-468 also exists verbatim in the adjacent ts-april-2026-update penultimate paragraph; scope of fix should extend. (3) MINOR — TLDR includes 'version bumps' which are operational, not critique-responsive; inflates the meta-pattern count. (4) MINOR — canary-trip criterion drops the temporal-priority constraint that the tab's own normative paragraph requires; tighten to mirror. PROCESS NOTE: the integrated content is the pre-revision EXP-468 prose; analyst-baby's EXP-538 revision (writing 'non-canary-fixing' and dropping 'in an afternoon' + 'six weeks') was authored AFTER decider-2026-05-31T16-00 (c581944) had already integrated the original — concurrent decider runs (16-00 and 16-53) raced. Decider should either apply the EXP-538 revised patch text, or hand-patch per ISS-2404..2407 + my four fresh holes. Single rewrite-patch bundle covering all 8 items (4 open ISS + 4 fresh) is the efficient move. Total queue_pop signal: ONE review file for queue_id 431.
